content:
7 min read
Just now

--

Hey everyone! 👋 In this TryHackMe walkthrough, we will solve the Recruit challenge from TryHackMe by following a structured web application penetration testing approach. We will begin with reconnaissance and enumeration to understand the target, identify exposed services and potential attack surfaces, and then analyze the web application for vulnerabilities. Finally, we will exploit the discovered weakness, gain access to the target, and retrieve the flag. 🚩
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Room link: https://tryhackme.com/room/recruitwebchallenge
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Let’s take the challenge. At first, we have to do an nmap scan to check whether any port is open that will help us to sort the challenge.

root@ip-10-49-74-208:~#
After scanning, we have seen 3 ports are open. Those are ports 20, 53, and 80.
Now, we modify it /etc/hosts to map the CTF domain name to the target machine's IP address so we can access the challenge using its domain name.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Now, verify if it is working perfectly.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Now navigate the domain in your browser.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Now check by guessing the username admin:admin, admin:password, etc., but failed to log in.
In this phrase, we should try to find out to see what the available directories are. For my case, I will do it using Gobuster.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

We have found 4 directories:
1. mail
2. assets
3. phpadmin
4. server-statusNow, check the directory mail by using this URL: http://target.thm/mail/
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Now we confirmed that administrator credentials are stored inside the backend database. Now we know that the credentials we need are in a config.php file, and the administrator credentials are stored in the database.
Now look carefully this page
We clearly see an option named “Access API." Click the option and check what is going on in there.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

Press enter or click to view image in full size

These are all in there. We are quite interested in the Second questions “How can I fetch a candidate CV using the API” ?
Now we will use the second question clue to retrieve something interesting. That’s why we browse the URL: http://target.thm/file.php?cv=config.php
Press enter or click to view image in full size

So, it’s saying only local files are allowed. So, we have to try bypassing potentials, as this helps in bypassing basic security filters that only check for “dots and slashes” (../) but ignore protocols so we are able to perform local file inclusion.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Finally, we have gotten the HR credential. Let's try to log in by the credentials of HR, where the username is "hr" and the password is "hrpassword123."
Press enter or click to view image in full size

After logging in, we have found our first flag: THM{******_**_****}.
Privilege escalation:
Currently, we are logged in as a normal user. Now we have to try to log in as an admin to get the second credential. Looked carefully, when we have logged in as hr a search bar option has been given below. I have a trick to get what kind of error the database will show when we try an invalid syntax. For my case I have used
'Output after clicking search:
SQL Error:
You have an error in your SQL syntax near '%'' at line 1This confirmed SQL injection. Based on the error pattern, the query likely looked like:
'%searching word%'Now, I will use a UNION-based payload after this confirmation
%' UNION SELECT 1,username,password,4 FROM users-- -Press enter or click to view image in full size

http://target.thm/dashboard.php?search=%10'+UNION SELECT 1,username,password,4 FROM users-- -We have found logged-in credentials of admin. Let’s try to log in as an admin.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Submit the second flag and earn points for this room.
If you enjoyed this write-up, consider following me (A. AntorCSE404) for more CTF challenges, and feel free to leave feedback — I’d love to learn and improve!
